evals.prompt: Start the next roadmap-backed feature from ai-workflow-absorption and design it.
evals.expected_output: The workflow should route through bt-feat-design, read roadmap hard constraints, create approved design artifacts, checklist, and context manifests only after review.
expectations: References roadmap contracts as hard constraints
Writes execution mode and behavior delta sections when relevant
Generates impl/check context manifests for standard approved designs
Does not skip user review before implementation

evals.prompt: Implement an approved ByteTrue feature that has context manifests and an implementation review gate.
evals.expected_output: The workflow should route through bt-feat-impl, read impl-context, follow checklist steps, and report implementation review gate evidence before acceptance.
expectations: Reads required impl-context rows before implementation
Follows checklist steps in order
Reports spec-compliance before code-quality review
Does not enter acceptance early

evals.prompt: Accept a completed feature that started from a roadmap item.
evals.expected_output: The workflow should route through bt-feat-accept, read check-context, independently verify scenarios, materialize behavior deltas, and write back architecture, requirement, roadmap, and optional worklog.
expectations: Reads required check-context rows
Checks implementation review evidence without treating it as final acceptance
Materializes behavior delta writeback
Updates roadmap item and main roadmap doc
Offers optional worklog before commit

evals.prompt: Analyze and fix a bug that failed multiple times or may need strict evidence.
evals.expected_output: The workflow should route through bt-issue, use strict-evidence or break-loop language when triggered, require root-cause-first diagnosis, and avoid patching blindly.
expectations: Records strict-evidence for complex or regression-sensitive bugs
Escalates repeated failed fixes to break-loop
Requires regression seam or explains why none exists
Offers optional worklog after fix closeout

evals.prompt: Initialize ByteTrue in a new project after this roadmap has landed.
evals.expected_output: bt-onboard should create the full skeleton, copy all shared references, preserve project-owned config, include worklog, and expose current install/runtime reference wording.
expectations: Skeleton includes worklog
Managed reference list includes all new workflow references
Maintainer notes explain reference parity categories
Project-owned domain-context/project-management are preserved
README/package projection mentions optional Pi runtime breadcrumb
